title: Helsing v2: Post-Quantum Decentralised Anonymous Payment with Isometry-Preserving Extraction
author: Stephenson, MJ
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
We present Helsing v2, a post-quantum decentralized anonymous payment protocol with a fully machine-verified security specification. The formalization, comprising 20 Lean 4 modules (~5,455 lines, zero sorry), establishes two main technical contributions: (i) isometry-preserving extraction—a norm-based framework that proves short-witness extraction and that every domain-switching transformation (NTT basis change, BARG permutation) preserves the shortness invariant required for Module-SIS security; and (ii) a complete hybrid security proof reducing all twelve protocol security properties to three base hardness assumptions (Module-LWE, Module-SIS, SHA-3 collision resistance), with explicit, quantified reduction losses. 
 All security reductions ground out in Module-LWE at (n = 256, k = 3, q = 3329) providing ≥166 quantum bits, Module-SIS at (n = 256, k = 6, q = 8380417) providing ≥165 quantum bits, and SHA-3 collision resistance providing ≥128 quantum bits. No elliptic-curve, pairing, or discrete-logarithm assumption is used. The Lean 4 source is available as supplementary material.
steps_raw:
We present Helsing v2, a post-quantum decentralized anonymous payment protocol with a fully machine-verified security specification. The formalization, comprising 20 Lean 4 modules (~5,455 lines, zero sorry), establishes two main technical contributions: (i) isometry-preserving extraction—a norm-based framework that proves short-witness extraction and that every domain-switching transformation (NTT basis change, BARG permutation) preserves the shortness invariant required for Module-SIS security; and (ii) a complete hybrid security proof reducing all twelve protocol security properties to three base hardness assumptions (Module-LWE, Module-SIS, SHA-3 collision resistance), with explicit, quantified reduction losses. 
 All security reductions ground out in Module-LWE at (n = 256, k = 3, q = 3329) providing ≥166 quantum bits, Module-SIS at (n = 256, k = 6, q = 8380417) providing ≥165 quantum bits, and SHA-3 collision resistance providing ≥128 quantum bits. No elliptic-curve, pairing, or discrete-logarithm assumption is used. The Lean 4 source is available as supplementary material.
